title: LCL PWM Destrudo Etc Split Was Kaworu really Adam
display_title: LCL PWM Destrudo Etc Split Was Kaworu really Adam
wikitext:
[]: LCL-PWM-Destrudo-Etc [Split: Was Kaworu really Adam?]             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jan 05, 2009 4:41 pm Split from here due to its mutation into a potpourri thread with no direct relevance to original topic. -Reichu It seems to me that a Contact Experiment with a SoL might obtain in both cases. DNA dives into SoL Things Happen Out pops an SoL, and a "clone" human - the donation gets used up From a CE with Adam and _____ we get Adam and a Kaworu. It would be nice if the unbalanced hour-glass widget we see "floating on water" after 2I were an Adam embryo and a Kaworu embryo. From a CE with Lilith/01 and Yui we get a deformed Lilith and a Rei. It seems Lilith and 01 may have been still connected at the time of the CE. That might explain why Lilith is still pretty discombobulated after 01 detaches, and perhaps speak to the disorganization of her leg regeneration prior to the LnY being removed. It wouldn't distress me if Rei and Kaworu had their own souls at "birth" and then had them transferred later. This helps explain why the Rei-quarium is filled with incarnate Rei-forms - they all have their own soul (as undeveloped as they may be, owing their entire experience in swimming in a tank). It suggests that Kaworu's original soul may at some point be in the Adam embryo. It suggests that a Rei soul could be in 00. I would have no trouble seeing Tabris being the instance of Kaworu with Adam's soul. The Dead Seas Scrolls are, after all, in an ancient language that probably didn't have words for "clone" and such. Kaworu explains his synch rates with saying that he can control any Adam-based Eva (if that Eva's soul is "hiding").

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jan 05, 2009 5:57 pm This helps explain why the Rei-quarium is filled with incarnate Rei-forms - they all have their own soul (as undeveloped as they may be, owing their entire experience in swimming in a tank).

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jan 05, 2009 6:26 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Ritsuko says otherwise; they have none.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jan 06, 2009 12:59 am Simple: They're PWM, which doesn't need souls/AT Fields to keep shape. Problem solved.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Jan 07, 2009 1:40 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Simple: They're PWM, which doesn't need souls/AT Fields to keep shape. Problem solved.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 07, 2009 1:50 pm Do we really know that non-pwm beings need a soul to maintain their shape though? Yes, crazy quantum magic beings yanking the soul out seems to cause a sudden loss of shape, but that would constitute an unusual circumstance, no?

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Jan 07, 2009 3:13 pm NemZ wrote: Do we really know that non-pwm beings need a soul to maintain their shape though? Yes, crazy quantum magic beings yanking the soul out seems to cause a sudden loss of shape, but that would constitute an unusual circumstance, no?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 07, 2009 3:44 pm But that's Rei, who is hardly an agreeable example of an average person. It actually seems that Rei might be the only person who requires an ATF to maintain her form (or maybe just to maintain her human form). Even Kaworu falls in chunks, not drips, when he is smooshed while his ATF is canceled out. As to the rest, I find the CI files highly questionable at times. Not only is their origin in question, but the method of delivery as contradictory notes at various levels of intrigue undermines the whole of it as a reliable source.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 07, 2009 3:51 pm Um, the CI is intentionally written to have contradictory information at the different levels. The highest level with In-Depth Information is the real scoop, what's really going on. For example: Evapedia Translation for CI from Evangelions (Evangelion 2) wrote: Adam A. Publicly Released Information Data unavailable. B. Generally Recognized Information The First Angel. The white giant crucified in Terminal Dogma. C. Confidential Information The entity, a progenitor of life, implanted into Earth by the First Ancestral Race. She was discovered at the South Pole by the Katsuragi Team, but, during the investigation, she blew apart and degenerated due to her S^2 Engine artificially going out of control. D. In-Depth Information There hasn't been a soul within Adam. At the time of Second Impact, her body was torn apart, and her soul flew off somewhere as a result. It was later recovered by Seele and incarnated, taking the form called Kaworu Nagisa. Because the Evas are a part of Kaworu's own body, he can manipulate them freely. However, he cannot go into them, for the reason that there are souls (the pilots' mothers) within the bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 07, 2009 3:52 pm NemZ wrote: Do we really know that non-pwm beings need a soul to maintain their shape though? Yes, crazy quantum magic beings yanking the soul out seems to cause a sudden loss of shape, but that would constitute an unusual circumstance, no?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 07, 2009 7:09 pm SSD: Yes, but people occasionally list things from the 'lower' levels as if it were fact, and if I recall correctly even the 'upper' levels of the CI have some internal contradictions... and none of this is on a high tier of evidence anyway. As far as I'm concerned, CI should rate below Rule of Cool. Reichu: Granted it is an important difference how the soul gets out, but the salient point was that this doesn't happen without the intervention of god-beings for whom the laws of physics seem to not apply anyway, thus making anything observed due to their intervention not exactly reliable for a more normal understanding of the nature of physical existence.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 07, 2009 7:24 pm Shinji turned to LCL in episode 19/20 without the aid of Rei/Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jan 07, 2009 10:04 pm Unit 01 is a clone of Lilith. That's probably got something to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 07, 2009 10:11 pm Yui/01 does all manner of seemingly impossible things throughout the series even before getting the s2 (does it really even do anything?) and ultimately 'defeats' Lilith in EOE. Yes, I include her as a god-being. If we got a view of Kagi turning into a puddle of tang after being shot by some random goon then we would have some useful observations... but dead things don't tang on their own. Something unexplained has to happen for that occur, and at that point it might as well just be called magic.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jan 08, 2009 1:14 am As far as I'm concerned, CI should rate below Rule of Cool.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:32 am AuraTwilight wrote: Duel of the Seeds. Gendo is the Donor. Shinji is a Furry. TEST TUBE BABY Asuka!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:32 am AuraTwilight wrote: Contrary to what religion says, apparently in the Evaverse, souls just sit in their dead bodies, since NERV is apparently able to salvage souls from the dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:39 am NemZ wrote: My point was that the CI seems to me just an example of furious fanwanking commissioned to fill a perceived need in the game for achievement rewards by Anno who had access to the early (dropped) script work and proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:42 am CI is more like "canonwanking", amirite?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 08, 2009 4:10 am Ornette wrote: fixed
